commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c3db53d5c920c1a6e993447236da18816e1dfdd3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves `install_homebrew` in `scripts/install.sh` into the missing-or-outdated Node branch and updates one installer-order test regex.

PR surface: Tests 0, Other 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main calls `install_homebrew` before `check_node`, and the PR-created missing-Git branch can reach `brew install git` without Homebrew. I did not run a live non-admin macOS installer reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer revision is needed because the branch has a concrete macOS installer blocker and still needs real patched-installer proof.

target.title: fix(install): skip Homebrew when adequate Node is already present
target.body:
## Summary

`install.sh` calls `install_homebrew` unconditionally before checking whether Node.js is already available. On macOS without admin rights and without Homebrew, this aborts the entire installation even when the existing Node satisfies the minimum version requirement (22.19+).

The user sees:

```
[1/3] Preparing environment
· Homebrew not found, installing
· Installing Homebrew
✗ Installing Homebrew failed
Need sudo access on macOS (e.g. the user needs to be an Administrator)!
```

The workaround (`npm i -g openclaw@latest`) works, confirming brew is not needed when Node is present.

## Fix

Move `check_node` before `install_homebrew` so the Homebrew step is only reached when Node actually needs to be installed. Users with an adequate Node on PATH skip straight to the npm install stage.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** install.sh runs install_homebrew unconditionally before checking whether Node.js is already present, blocking non-admin macOS users who already have Node >= 22.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.5, Node v26.0.0, openclaw main.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  bash -c '
if command -v node >/dev/null && node -e "process.exit(parseInt(process.version.slice(1))>=22?0:1)"; then
  echo "Node $(node -v) >= 22 detected BEFORE Homebrew step"
  echo "Result: Homebrew installation skipped entirely"
else
  echo "Node not found or < 22 — would install Homebrew first"
fi
'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  ```
  === Proof: skip Homebrew when adequate Node present ===

Current Node: v26.0.0

The fix reorders install.sh main():
  BEFORE: install_homebrew -> check_node -> install_node
  AFTER:  check_node -> (only if needed) install_homebrew -> install_node

Node v26.0.0 >= 22 detected BEFORE Homebrew step
Result: Homebrew installation skipped entirely
On a machine with Node 22+ from nvm/fnm/volta, brew is never invoked.
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** install_homebrew moved inside the check_node guard. With Node 26 on PATH, the Homebrew step is never reached. Non-admin macOS users with adequate Node skip straight to npm install.
- **What was not tested:** Live non-admin macOS install (requires account without sudo).
target.author: sebtardif
